## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

### Utilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Utilities")]

**Electricity**

Electricity for the Gold Coast is sourced from Powerlink Queensland at bulk supply substations which is provided via the [National Electricity Market](/wiki/National_Electricity_Market "National Electricity Market") from an interconnected multi-State power system. The Government-owned electricity corporation [Energex](/wiki/Energex "Energex") distributes and retails electricity, natural gas, [liquefied petroleum gas](/wiki/Liquefied_petroleum_gas "Liquefied petroleum gas") (LPG) and value-added products and services to residential, industrial and commercial customers in South-East Queensland.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b5/Hinze_Dam_2019.11.08.jpg/250px-Hinze_Dam_2019.11.08.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hinze_Dam_2019.11.08.jpg)

[Hinze Dam](/wiki/Hinze_Dam "Hinze Dam")

**Water supply**

The [Hinze Dam](/wiki/Hinze_Dam "Hinze Dam") 15 km (9.3 mi) southwest of Nerang is the population's main water supply. The [Little Nerang Dam](/wiki/Little_Nerang_Dam "Little Nerang Dam") which feeds into [Hinze Dam](/wiki/Hinze_Dam "Hinze Dam") can supplement part of the city area's water needs, and both are managed by the city council directorate [Gold Coast Water](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Water "Gold Coast Water"). Reforms of the way in which the [water industry](/wiki/Water_industry "Water industry") is structured have been announced by the State Government, with transfer of ownership and management of water services from local government to the state occurring in 2008–09. [City of Gold Coast](/wiki/City_of_Gold_Coast "City of Gold Coast") also sources water from [Wivenhoe Dam](/wiki/Wivenhoe_Dam "Wivenhoe Dam"), west of Brisbane for northern suburbs when the Hinze Dam, at one-tenth of Wivenhoe's capacity, becomes low.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Water shortage and water restrictions have been current local issues, and a few new Gold Coast residential areas have recently included dual reticulation in their planning and development to supply water from a new water recycling plant being built concurrently. This will make available highly treated recycled water for use around the home in addition to potable water. The Gold Coast has received world recognition for this scheme in its Pimpama-Coomera suburbs.[[118]](#cite_note-118)

Gold Coast Water has the capacity to supply up to 133 megalitres of desalinated water per day.[[119]](#cite_note-119)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Transport")]

Main article: [Transport on the Gold Coast, Queensland](/wiki/Transport_on_the_Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Transport on the Gold Coast, Queensland")

The car is the dominant mode of transport in the Gold Coast, with over 70% of people using it as their sole mode of travelling to work.[[120]](#cite_note-120) The Gold Coast has an extensive network of arterial roads that link coastal suburbs with inland suburbs. In recent years, local and state governments have invested money in transport infrastructure on the Gold Coast to combat the increasing congestion on many of the city's roads. The Gold Coast has an extensive public transport network that includes buses, heavy rail and light rail for commuting to work, visiting attractions, and travelling to other destinations.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/68/Skylines_of_Main_Beach_and_Surfers_Paradise%2C_Queensland_in_January_2017_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Skylines_of_Main_Beach_and_Surfers_Paradise%2C_Queensland_in_January_2017_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Skylines_of_Main_Beach_and_Surfers_Paradise,_Queensland_in_January_2017_(cropped).jpg)

The [Gold Coast Highway](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Highway "Gold Coast Highway") and [G:link](/wiki/G:link "G:link") crossing the [Nerang River](/wiki/Nerang_River "Nerang River")

### Road

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Road")]

A number of major roads connect the Gold Coast with Brisbane, New South Wales, and the surrounding areas. The [Pacific Motorway](/wiki/Pacific_Motorway_(Brisbane%E2%80%93Brunswick_Heads) "Pacific Motorway (Brisbane–Brunswick Heads)") (M1) is the main motorway in the area. Beginning at the [Logan Motorway](/wiki/Logan_Motorway "Logan Motorway") (M6) in Brisbane, it travels through the inland Gold Coast region and links with the [Pacific Highway](/wiki/Pacific_Highway_(Australia) "Pacific Highway (Australia)") at the New South Wales/Queensland border near [Tweed Heads](/wiki/Tweed_Heads,_New_South_Wales "Tweed Heads, New South Wales"). The Pacific Motorway is part of the [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane") to [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") corridor. Before the [Tugun Bypass](/wiki/Tugun_Bypass "Tugun Bypass") was completed in 2008, the motorway ended at [Tugun](/wiki/Tugun,_Queensland "Tugun, Queensland"). The [Gold Coast Highway](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Highway "Gold Coast Highway") services the coastal suburbs of the Gold Coast, including Surfers Paradise, Southport, and Burleigh Heads. Starting at the Pacific Motorway at Tweed Heads, it runs parallel to the coast until it reaches Labrador, where it turns inland to meet the Pacific Motorway again at [Helensvale](/wiki/Helensvale "Helensvale"). Other arterial roads include the [Smith Street Motorway](/wiki/Smith_Street_Motorway "Smith Street Motorway") which connects Southport, Gold Coast's CBD with the [M1](/wiki/Pacific_Motorway_(Brisbane%E2%80%93Brunswick_Heads) "Pacific Motorway (Brisbane–Brunswick Heads)") in [Parkwood](/wiki/Parkwood,_Queensland "Parkwood, Queensland"). Other major roads include Reedy Creek Road, [Nerang–Broadbeach Road](/wiki/Nerang%E2%80%93Broadbeach_Road "Nerang–Broadbeach Road"), [Robina Parkway](/wiki/Robina_Parkway "Robina Parkway") and [Southport–Burleigh Road](/wiki/Southport_%E2%80%93_Burleigh_Road "Southport – Burleigh Road").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Light rail

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Light rail")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0f/GCLR_Set_9_at_Cypress_Avenue_2014-09-28.jpg/250px-GCLR_Set_9_at_Cypress_Avenue_2014-09-28.jpg)](/wiki/File:GCLR_Set_9_at_Cypress_Avenue_2014-09-28.jpg)

[G:link](/wiki/G:link "G:link") trams on Cypress Avenue

The Gold Coast's light rail service is called [G:link](/wiki/G:link "G:link"), a 20 km (12 mi) line between [Helensvale](/wiki/Helensvale,_Queensland "Helensvale, Queensland") and [Broadbeach](/wiki/Broadbeach,_Queensland "Broadbeach, Queensland") that also connects the key activity centres of [Southport](/wiki/Southport,_Queensland "Southport, Queensland") and [Surfers Paradise](/wiki/Surfers_Paradise,_Queensland "Surfers Paradise, Queensland"). The G:link was opened in 2014 between Broadbeach and Southport, with an extension to Helensvale completed in 2017 in preparation for the [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games").[[121]](#cite_note-121)

### Heavy rail

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Heavy rail")]

[Queensland Rail](/wiki/Queensland_Rail "Queensland Rail") operates an inter-city rail service from Brisbane to the Gold Coast along the [Gold Coast railway line](/wiki/Gold_Coast_railway_line "Gold Coast railway line"). The line follows the same route as Brisbane's [Beenleigh railway line](/wiki/Beenleigh_railway_line "Beenleigh railway line"), continuing on after reaching [Beenleigh](/wiki/Beenleigh_railway_station "Beenleigh railway station"). It then follows a route similar to that of the Pacific Motorway, passing stations at [Ormeau](/wiki/Ormeau_railway_station "Ormeau railway station"), [Coomera](/wiki/Coomera_railway_station "Coomera railway station"), [Helensvale](/wiki/Helensvale_railway_station "Helensvale railway station"), [Nerang](/wiki/Nerang_railway_station "Nerang railway station") and [Robina](/wiki/Robina_railway_station "Robina railway station") before terminating at [Varsity Lakes](/wiki/Varsity_Lakes_railway_station "Varsity Lakes railway station"). An extension of the Gold Coast line to the Gold Coast Airport is proposed.[[122]](#cite_note-122)

### Bus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Bus")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/Gold_Coast_buses_%2828259988216%29.jpg/250px-Gold_Coast_buses_%2828259988216%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Gold_Coast_buses_(28259988216).jpg)

[Kinetic Gold Coast](/wiki/Kinetic_Gold_Coast "Kinetic Gold Coast") buses operating in [Broadbeach](/wiki/Broadbeach "Broadbeach")

[Kinetic Gold Coast](/wiki/Kinetic_Gold_Coast "Kinetic Gold Coast")[[123]](#cite_note-123)[[124]](#cite_note-124) operates all public passenger services in the city under contract by [Translink](/wiki/Translink_(Queensland) "Translink (Queensland)") which coordinates the public transport network in [South East Queensland](/wiki/South_East_Queensland "South East Queensland"). Services are frequent during the day, with intervals being as little as 5 minutes. Kinetic operates over a fleet of over 400 buses operating on over 70 lines covering the entire city.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Airports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Airports")]

The main International Airport, [Gold Coast Airport](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Airport "Gold Coast Airport") is located in Coolangatta, approximately 22 kilometres (14 mi) south of Surfers Paradise. Services are provided to interstate capitals and major cities as well as to major New Zealand cities, Malaysia, Japan, Indonesia and Singapore.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In 2019, it was the [sixth busiest airport](/wiki/List_of_the_busiest_airports_in_Australia "List of the busiest airports in Australia") in Australia.[[125]](#cite_note-125)

Smaller private airfields are located in the northern suburbs of [Coombabah](/wiki/Coombabah "Coombabah") ([Southport Airport](/wiki/Southport_Airport "Southport Airport")) and [Norwell](/wiki/Norwell,_Queensland "Norwell, Queensland") ([Heck Field](/wiki/Heck_Field "Heck Field")), catering to flight training, recreational and [general aviation](/wiki/General_aviation "General aviation") users.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Real estate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gold_Coast,_Queensland&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Real estate")]

The Gold Coast's real estate market is one of the most dynamic in Australia, characterised by a combination of coastal luxury properties, residential developments, and investment opportunities. According to data from SQM Research,[[130]](#cite_note-130) the region has experienced significant property value growth in recent years, with both the median house and unit prices showing strong upward trends. The market's appeal is driven by its lifestyle offerings, proximity to major cities like Brisbane, and ongoing infrastructure improvements.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
